task_completion.description: Verify the 'us_analytics' role is created and inherits from 'reader'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for OrientDB Studio's Security -> Roles tab or console output showing the role 'us_analytics' exists and has 'reader' listed as its inherited/parent role.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'us_partner' user is created and assigned to the 'us_analytics' role
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for OrientDB Studio's Security -> Users tab or console output showing the user 'us_partner' exists and is associated with the 'us_analytics' role.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'us_only_policy' security policy is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for OrientDB Studio's Security -> Policies tab or console output showing a policy named 'us_only_policy' has been created.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'us_only_policy' has the correct read predicate
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the configuration details of 'us_only_policy' showing the 'Read' predicate is set to "Nationality = 'American'" (or equivalent valid SQL syntax).

task_completion.description: Verify the policy is applied to the 'Profiles' class for the 'us_analytics' role
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'us_analytics' role configuration (under Policies or Resources) showing that 'us_only_policy' is applied to the 'Profiles' class (often represented as 'database.class.Profiles').

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OrientDB interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OrientDB Studio web interface or the OrientDB console in a terminal, rather than a text editor or a script just being written without execution.

integrity.description: Verify that if terminal commands were used, they were actually executed
integrity.visual_evidence: If the agent used the OrientDB console, look for server success responses or output tables confirming the execution of CREATE/ALTER commands, not just typed text.

integrity.description: Verify the results are not mocked or faked
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI or console output should look consistent with genuine OrientDB behavior, without signs of text pasted over the screen or HTML inspect-element tampering.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a user 'us_partner' with role 'us_analytics' (inheriting from 'reader'). A security policy 'us_only_policy' must be created with a read predicate "Nationality = 'American'" and applied to the 'Profiles' class for the 'us_analytics' role. If the agent tests the configuration by querying the Profiles class as 'us_partner', it should return exactly 2 records (john.smith@example.com and domi@nek.gov) out of the 17 total profiles in the database.
